mnemonics: Zero-One Matrices: A friend Owns Nice Things Everyday (AONTE)
Directed Graphs: Dogs Interpret Names, People Understand (DINPU)
Partial Orders: Horses Eat Nice Things Incredibly Regularly (HENIR)
Equivalence Relations: Having Elegant Understanding Qualities In Genuine Relationships (HEUQIR)
keyPoints: A relation is reflexive, symmetric, and transitive if it is an equivalence relation.
A zero-one matrix is used to represent a relation in a computer program.
A directed graph is a graph where each edge has a direction.
A partial order is a relation that is reflexive, antisymmetric, and transitive.
A Hasse diagram is a graphical representation of a partial order.
Equivalence relations partition a set into disjoint subsets, called equivalence classes.
